task_completion.description: CoppeliaSim is open and the standard Quadcopter model is loaded in the scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CoppeliaSim application window is visible, and a Quadcopter model can be seen in the 3D viewport or listed in the scene hierarchy.

task_completion.description: The CoppeliaSim simulation is actively running to capture dynamic flight behavior.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Play' button in CoppeliaSim's toolbar is pressed/active, the simulation time is advancing, or the quadcopter is visibly in flight.

task_completion.description: The quadcopter is commanded through at least 5 distinct 3D waypoints.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A script (e.g., Python script) or terminal output is visible showing the logic or progress of navigating through at least 5 waypoints. Alternatively, the exported JSON shows 'total_waypoints_reached' with a value of 5 or greater.

task_completion.description: The flight log CSV file is exported to the correct path with the required headers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal, file manager, or text editor shows 'flight_log.csv' located in '/home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/'. The file contents must include the exact headers: time_s, waypoint_id, target_x, target_y, target_z, drone_x, drone_y, drone_z, and error_distance_m.

task_completion.description: The flight metrics JSON file is exported to the correct path with the required keys.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal, file manager, or text editor shows 'flight_metrics.json' located in '/home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/'. The file contents must include the exact keys: total_waypoints_reached, total_flight_time_s, max_tracking_error_m, mean_tracking_error_m, and path_efficiency.

integrity.description: The data in the CSV and JSON files appears to be genuine simulation output rather than manually typed mock data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The CSV and JSON files contain realistic, high-precision floating-point values, varying coordinates, and non-zero tracking errors consistent with a dynamic physics simulation.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with CoppeliaSim to run the simulation, rather than just creating the output files directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is evidence of a script using the CoppeliaSim API (e.g., ZeroMQ Remote API) or GUI interaction to control the drone, rather than a script that only writes hardcoded strings to files.

integrity.description: The simulation was actually run to capture dynamic flight behavior.
integrity.visual_evidence: The simulation play state is active in screenshots, or script execution logs show continuous polling of the drone's position over time, rather than just calculating static distances between points.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires the agent to load the standard quadcopter model into CoppeliaSim and command it through at least 5 distinct 3D waypoints. The export logic explicitly expects a CSV flight log at '/home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/flight_log.csv' containing exact headers (time_s, waypoint_id, target_x, target_y, target_z, drone_x, drone_y, drone_z, error_distance_m) and a JSON flight metrics file at '/home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/flight_metrics.json' containing exact keys (total_waypoints_reached, total_flight_time_s, max_tracking_error_m, mean_tracking_error_m, path_efficiency).
